title: artifact: display :latest tag in ls output when no explicit tag was given
body:
Fixes #27083

## Problem

`podman artifact add quay.io/myimage/myartifact /tmp/foobar.txt` without
an explicit tag shows an empty TAG column in `podman artifact ls`:

```
REPOSITORY                        TAG  DIGEST        SIZE
quay.io/myimage/myartifact             9f6c351a7733  637.7 MB
```

Container images default to `:latest` in the same situation.

## Root Cause

`list.go`'s `outputTemplate` function parses the stored artifact name with
`reference.Parse()`, which does **not** add a default tag. The tag is then
extracted only if the parsed reference implements `reference.Tagged`. Since no
tag is present, `tag` stays as the empty string `""`.

Meanwhile, `NewArtifactReference` (which is called during `artifact add`)
already applies `reference.TagNameOnly()` on the way in, ensuring `:latest` is
stored in the backing index. The display code was just not applying the same
normalisation on the way out.

## Fix

Apply `reference.TagNameOnly(named)` after the initial parse so the extracted
tag is always `:latest` when no explicit tag is present — consistent with
container image behaviour.

## Before / After

**Before:**
```
REPOSITORY                        TAG  DIGEST        SIZE
quay.io/myimage/myartifact             9f6c351a7733  637.7 MB
```

**After:**
```
REPOSITORY                        TAG     DIGEST        SIZE
quay.io/myimage/myartifact        latest  9f6c351a7733  637.7 MB
```

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
